commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=bf39b5507f6efc88b7d560f61941e6275aac23ba)
commands.review_summary:
The branch origin-couples `/auth/docs` loopback return origins, removes loopback from Vercel `form-action`, adds docs-auth tests, and records the token-destination invariant.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main allows loopback return origins and the route posts the auth token to the computed callback. I did not run the full authenticated browser flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because this is a member-authored P0 token-destination security change with a compatibility break and missing contributor-owned runtime proof.

target.title: fix: restrict docs auth return origins to dev only
target.body:
## Summary

`/auth/docs` (Ask Molty) signs a user in and auto-POSTs their ClawHub auth token to the `return_to` origin. The allowlist trusted `http://localhost:4173` / `http://127.0.0.1:4173` **unconditionally**, so in production a user lured to `/auth/docs?return_to=http://localhost:4173/...` has their bearer token POSTed to whatever local process holds that port. This is the `/auth/docs` half of **GHSA-mp85-24m3-52jg**.

The fix couples loopback trust to the app's own origin: a loopback `return_to` is allowed **only when the app itself is served from loopback**, so a public/staging/preview deployment can never hand the token to localhost — regardless of `NODE_ENV` or build mode.

## What Changed

- `src/lib/docsAuth.ts` — replace the flat allowlist with origin-coupling. Production docs origins (`clawhub.ai`, `documentation.openclaw.ai`, `docs.openclaw.ai`) are always allowed; a loopback `return_to` is allowed only when `currentOrigin` is also loopback. On server render `currentOrigin` is unknown (`null`), so loopback is rejected there too.
- `vercel.json` — drop loopback origins from the production CSP `form-action`, so the browser blocks a cross-origin form POST to localhost even if the app allowlist ever regressed. The two layers must stay aligned.
- `specs/auth-identity.md` — record the docs-auth token-destination invariant and the CSP-alignment requirement.
- `src/lib/docsAuth.test.ts` — regression coverage for the matrix below.

This restores the reviewed origin-coupled implementation that briefly existed on main (`e86aa30a`) before the docs-subdomain move reverted it, and re-adds `https://clawhub.ai`.

## Flow (before → after)

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  subgraph After["AFTER — origin-coupled"]
    A2[return_to] --> B2{production docs origin?}
    B2 -- yes --> OK2[ALLOW]
    B2 -- no --> C2{return_to loopback?}
    C2 -- no --> NO2[REJECT]
    C2 -- yes --> D2{app origin loopback?}
    D2 -- yes --> OKL[ALLOW local-to-local]
    D2 -- no --> PUB[REJECT: public app cannot POST token to localhost]
  end
  subgraph Before["BEFORE — flat allowlist"]
    A1[return_to] --> B1{in fixed set incl. localhost:4173?}
    B1 -- yes --> OK1[ALLOW — even in production]
    B1 -- no --> NO1[REJECT]
  end
```

## Behavior

| `return_to` | Before (any env) | After @ public origin | After @ loopback origin |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `http://localhost:4173/...` | ALLOWED | **REJECTED** | ALLOWED |
| `http://127.0.0.1:4173/...` | ALLOWED | **REJECTED** | ALLOWED |
| `https://docs.openclaw.ai/...` | ALLOWED | ALLOWED | ALLOWED |
| `https://clawhub.ai/...` | n/a (absent) | ALLOWED | ALLOWED |
| `https://evil.example.com/...` | REJECTED | REJECTED | REJECTED |

## Runtime proof (real browser)

Playwright drove the **real shipped `src/lib/docsAuth.ts`** (served by a bare Vite that transpiles the actual module — no copy, no mock) at two real browser origins. `normalizeDocsReturnTo` reads the live `window.location.origin`, so each load exercises the real decision. Only the app origin differs between runs:

- **`http://127.0.2.2:5199`** (non-loopback hostname → prod-style): `localhost:4173` **REJECTED**, `127.0.0.1:4173` **REJECTED**, `docs.openclaw.ai` ALLOWED, `clawhub.ai` ALLOWED, `evil.example.com` REJECTED.
- **`http://127.0.0.1:5199`** (loopback → local dev): both loopback returns ALLOWED, prod origins ALLOWED, `evil.example.com` REJECTED.

Scope: this exercises the shipped origin-check module in a real browser at real origins. It is not the full authenticated `/auth/docs` POST — the local app SSR needs a Convex backend that isn't available here — but the origin check is the gate that decides whether the token-carrying form is ever built. Both runs were screenshotted; since the GitHub CLI can't upload images, a maintainer can drag-drop them inline if a visual is wanted — the matrix above is the same data.

## How To Test

```text
bun install
./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/lib/docsAuth.test.ts src/routes/auth/-docs.test.tsx
```

For the real-browser check: serve `src/lib/docsAuth.ts` via a bare Vite (no SSR), open the page at `http://127.0.0.1:<port>` (loopback app origin → loopback returns allowed) and `http://127.0.2.2:<port>` (non-loopback app origin → loopback returns rejected). `normalizeDocsReturnTo(returnTo)` reads `window.location.origin`.

## Verification

```text
vitest src/lib/docsAuth.test.ts + src/routes/auth/-docs.test.tsx  → 9 passed
tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json                                      → exit 0
oxlint --type-aware (changed files)                                → clean
oxfmt --check (changed files)                                      → clean
real-browser origin matrix (Playwright, shipped module)            → see Runtime proof
```

Rebased onto current `main`; single commit.
target.author: jesse-merhi

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
